name: +1 Intimidating Dagger of Acid
description:
Dripping with acid, this weapon gives its bearer a bonus to intimidate their
foes.
dropLocation: Special event items, The Festival of Olladra Contest

name: +1 Starter Dagger
description: A short simple weapon with a pointed blade used for stabbing.
dropLocation: The Voyage, End reward

name: +1 Starter Great Axe
description: A big, heavy, double-bladed axe wieldable only with two hands.
dropLocation: The Voyage, End reward

name: +1 Starter Greatsword
description:
The largest of the swords, this weapon can only be used with two hands. The
cross guard of this sword appears to have some engraving that was filed off.
dropLocation: The Voyage, End reward

name: +1 Starter Handwraps
description: Strips of cloth meant to be wrapped around your hands.
dropLocation: The Voyage, End reward

name: +1 Starter Heavy Mace
description:
A simple weapon comprised of a solid blunt head attached to a haft. A religious
symbol appears to have been defaced on the head of the weapon.
dropLocation: The Voyage, End reward

name: +1 Starter Kama
description:
A weapon with a straight inside edge attached perpendicularly to a thin wooden
haft and wielded with one hand. The wooden haft appears to have been sanded
down to remove a series of marks that would have made this item identifiable.
dropLocation: The Voyage, End reward

name: +1 Starter Longbow
description:
A large, curved wooden bow, often nearly as tall as a human. This weapon is
popular among elves and rangers. The bow appears to have once been carved with
a name that appears to have been made illegible by a chisel.
dropLocation: The Voyage, End reward

name: +1 Starter Longsword
description:
A classic straight-bladed sword usable in one hand. This weapon is commonly
associated with knighthood and valor. The pommel appears to have had a symbol
that has been battered with a hammer.
dropLocation: The Voyage, End reward

name: +1 Starter Quarterstaff
description:
A long, stout staff used as a simple weapon. Often favored by wizards or common
folk.
dropLocation: The Voyage, End reward

name: +1 Starter Rapier
description:
A long, thin sword with a fine point. Unlike other swords, this weapon lacks
an edged blade, and can only be used for thrusting attacks.
dropLocation: The Voyage, End reward

name: +1 Starter Scimitar
description:
A curved sword favored by the elves of Valenar. The unique shape gives this
weapon a keener edge. An enscription on the hilt reads 'To my dearest ...'.
The rest of the wording appears to have been melted off the hilt.
dropLocation: The Voyage, End reward

name: +1 Starter Shortbow
description: A smaller wooden bow with a shorter firing distance.
dropLocation: The Voyage, End reward

name: +1 Starter Shortsword
description:
The smallest of the swords, light enough to be used as an offhand weapon but
too large to be considered a dagger. This appears to have an emblem that has
been sanded off the hilt.
dropLocation: The Voyage, End reward

name: +1 Vorpal Sickle of Haggling
description:
Useful for haggling, the Vorpal Sickle is a recognized tool used in trading
caravans from the Dhakaani clans of Darguun.
dropLocation:
Special event items, 
Emerald Claw Conspiracy, September 2008
Trade or no Trade, March 30, 2007 - April 8, 2007

name: +2 Intimidating Dagger of Paralyzing Acid
description:
Dripping with acid, this weapon gives its bearer a bonus to intimidate their
foes, additionally the acid from this weapon has a paralyzing effect on those
injured by its blade.
dropLocation: Special event items, The Festival of Olladra Contest

name: +2 Starter Great Axe
description: A big, heavy, double-bladed axe wieldable only with two hands.
dropLocation: The Voyage, End reward

name: -1 Quarterstaff of Lesser Halfling Bane
description:
A long, stout staff used as a simple weapon. Often favored by wizards or common
folk.
dropLocation: Cannith Crafting Tutorial, End reward

name: Absolute Judgement
description: Only a mad scientist deals in absolutes.
dropLocation: The Underlane Assignment, End chest

name: Acera, the Dissolution of All
description:
Not content with bringing destruction to wherever it came from, after being
recovered from the mists, this hungry blade demands more.
dropLocation: Den of Vipers, end chest

name: Acrida, the Caustic Arbalest
description:
The mists seem to have rendered this bone crossbow immune to the caustic acids
that dwell within it.
dropLocation: Den of Vipers, end chest

name: Adamantine Knuckles (level 12)
description: These cloth handwraps hide a hard-hitting secret-adamantine knuckles underneath.
dropLocation: A Cabal for One, Feast or Famine, End chest (Heroic Normal)

name: Adamantine Knuckles (level 13)
description: These cloth handwraps hide a hard-hitting secret-adamantine knuckles underneath.
dropLocation: A Cabal for One, Feast or Famine, End chest (Hard)

name: Adamantine Knuckles (level 14)
description: These cloth handwraps hide a hard-hitting secret-adamantine knuckles underneath.
dropLocation: A Cabal for One, Feast or Famine, End chest (Elite)

name: Adamantine Knuckles (level 23)
description: These cloth handwraps hide a hard-hitting secret-adamantine knuckles underneath.
dropLocation: Return to Cabal for One, Feast or Famine, End chest (Epic Normal)

name: Adamantine Knuckles (level 24)
description: These cloth handwraps hide a hard-hitting secret-adamantine knuckles underneath.
dropLocation: Return to Cabal for One, Feast or Famine, End chest (Epic Hard)

name: Adamantine Knuckles (level 25)
description: These cloth handwraps hide a hard-hitting secret-adamantine knuckles underneath.
dropLocation: Return to Cabal for One, and Feast or Famine End chest (Epic Elite)

name: Adoryn's Malice
description:
This light mace was named after one of the workers that worked in the foundry
of the Twilight Forge thousands of years ago. Adoryn was responsible for running
the forge that birthed the Warforged Titan . Upon realizing what he had helped
unleash on the world, he attempted to destroy it with this specially designed,
handcrafted light mace.
dropLocation: The Twilight Forge, End Chest

name: Aeon, the Blazing Reign
description: The red dragon's resurrection was meant to herald in an age of fire.
dropLocation: Threats Old and New, Raid Warded chest

name: Aerania, the Boltbow
description:
The Eladrin of the Feywild crafted this exquisite bow of the finest of wood
and silver. The arrows it fires arc with an electricity that shine brightly
in the gloom.
dropLocation: The Dryad and the Demigod, End chest and optional chest

name: Agony, the Knife in the Dark
description: A blade such as this will always find a black heart to sheathe itself.
dropLocation: Caught in the Web, End Chest
_file: Agony, the Knife in the Dark.item

name: Alarphon's Staff
description:
This metal staff hums with power. Arcane runes are inscribed along its length.
In the hands of an arcane caster, this staff is capable of casting a diverse
array of offensive spells. Although this staff does not regain charges when
you rest, it recharges automatically as time passes.
dropLocation: Eveningstar, Turn in 15 Commendations: War Wizards Commendation: War Wizards : This is a valuable item that can be exchanged for goods if brought to a Challenge Trader. This is a token of favor earned by helping the War Wizards. You can exchange it in Eveningstar for powerful equipment.

name: Alchemical Bastard Sword
description:
Also known as a hand-and-a-half sword, and the largest of the single-handed
swords, this weapon requires special trainiing to use.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Battle Axe
description: A sturdy, broad-headed axe used as a weapon especially among dwarves.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Dagger
description: A short simple weapon with a pointed blade used for stabbing.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Dart
description:
A small weapon the size of an arrow, with a weighted head. Youre quite surpised
that House Cannith would bother having a recipe for darts..
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Dwarven Waraxe
description:
This axe is too large to be used in one hand without the special training that
all dwarven warriors receive.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Falchion
description:
This large, curved sword is essentially a scimitar design to be used with two
hands.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Great Crossbow
description:
The largest of the crossbows, it is weighty enough to require special training
to use.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End-chest

name: Alchemical Greataxe
description: A big, heavy, double-bladed axe wieldable only with two hands.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End Chest

name: Alchemical Greatclub
description:
A more formidable version of a simple club, this two-handed weapon is occasionally
reinforced with spikes or bands of iron.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Greatsword
description: The largest of the swords, this weapon can only be used with two hands.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Hand Axe
description: A smaller version of the Battleaxe, favored as an offhand weapon by dwarves.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End-chest

name: Alchemical Handwraps
description: Strips of cloth meant to be wrapped around your hands.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Heavy Crossbow
description:
A ranged weapon consisting of a small bow mounted crosswise on a heavy wooden
stock. This weapon is large enough to require a winch for reloading, yet is
surprisingly simple to use.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Heavy Mace
description: A simple weapon comprised of a solid blunt head attached to a haft.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Heavy Pick
description:
A hammer with a sharp, pointed head designed to concentrate the force of its
blow on a small area. Similar to a miner's pick, but reinforced for battle.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Kama
description:
A weapon with a straight inside edge attached perpendicularly to a thin wooden
haft and wielded with one hand.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Khopesh
description: An ancient sword with a heavy spine and a single blade.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Kukri
description: A heavy, curved knife with a sharp edge on the inside of the curve.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Light Crossbow
description:
A simple ranged weapon consisting of a small bow mounted crosswise on a heavy
wooden stock.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Light Hammer
description: A small sledge with a heavy blunt, square head.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Light Mace
description: A smaller simple weapon comprised of a solid blunt head attached to a haft.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Light Pick
description:
A smaller hammer with a sharp, pointed head designed to concentrate the force
of its blow on a small area. Similar to a miner's pick, but smaller and reinforced
for battle.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Longbow
description:
A large, curved wooden bow, often nearly as tall as a human. This weapon is
popular among elves and rangers.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Longsword
description:
A classic straight-bladed sword usable in one hand. This weapon is commonly
associated with knighthood and valor.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Maul
description:
The largest of the hammers, often created with a wedge-shaped head for splitting
logs.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Morningstar
description: A simple weapon comprised of a round, spiked head attached to a long haft.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Quarterstaff
description:
A long, stout staff used as a simple weapon. Often favored by wizards or common
folk.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Rapier
description:
A long, thin sword with a fine point. Unlike other swords, this weapon lacks
an edged blade, and can only be used for thrusting attacks.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Repeating Heavy Crossbow
description:
A complex weapon consisting of a heavy crossbow and a clip for bolts that is
loaded by pulling the lever.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Repeating Light Crossbow
description:
A complex weapon consisting of a light crossbow and a clip for bolts that is
loaded by pulling the reloading lever.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Sceptre
description: A simple weapon with a wide, round head and a narrow haft.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Scimitar
description:
A curved sword favored by the elves of Valenar. The unique shape gives this
weapon a keener edge.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Shortbow
description: A smaller wooden bow with a shorter firing distance.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Shortsword
description:
The smallest of the swords, light enough to be used as an offhand weapon, but
too large to be considered a dagger.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Shuriken
description:
A tiny circular throwing weapon with a serrated edge. Favored by the plentiful
ninjas of House Cannith.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Sickle
description:
A simple weapon with a semicircular blade attached to a short handle. Typically
used by farmers for cutting grain.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Throwing Axe
description: A small, light axe design for throwing.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Throwing Dagger
description: A short simple weapon with a pointed blade balanced for throwing.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Throwing Hammer
description: A small, light hammer designed for throwing.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Alchemical Warhammer
description:
A large sledge with a heavy, square head. Popular among dwarves as an alternative
to the axe.
dropLocation: The Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Allegiance (historic)
description:
A fine Khopesh enchanted to do battle against the rebellious elves in the last
days of the Lost Empire. Most likely, it was wielded by a loyal servant to
spill his own kin's blood.
dropLocation: Gianthold Tor, Black Dragon Chest

name: Amplin, the Channeling Bolt
description: A magical current runs end to end through this seemingly wooden staff.
dropLocation: Den of Vipers, end chest

name: Ancient Vulkoorim Dagger
description:
An ancient dagger forged in the days of the elven revolt against the giants.
This dagger has a small chance to kill any giant outright.
dropLocation: An Offering of Blood, any chest after 2nd Aspect

name: Antipode, Fist of the Horizon
description:
Spun from enchanted golden thread, this cloth was created to harness and contain
planer energies. When wrapped around ones hands, their unarmed attacks can
unleash those energies to devastating effect.
dropLocation: Caught in the Web, End Chest

name: Antique Greataxe
description:
Lamden Wynn of the Golden Wing Inn kept this greataxe behind the bar in case
of trouble. It's an old family heirloom, but he offered it to you as compensation
for your efforts.
dropLocation: The Maleficent Cabal, End reward

name: Arc Ricochet
description: Lightning flickers between the barbs on this shuriken.
dropLocation: Ruinous Schemes, End chest

name: Arcane Shard
description:
This staff is what remains of an ancient giant artifact that was said to imbue
the wielder with phenomenal magical power.
dropLocation: Dreams of Insanity, End Chest

name: Arctica, the Mystic Cold
description: The magic of a winter that seems unending is bound within this axe.
dropLocation: Den of Vipers, end chest

name: Arlyn's Staff
description:
A long, stout staff, engraved with the name 'Arlyn Feathercrest' and a displacer
beast.
dropLocation: Gwylan's Stand, Arlyn Feathercrest 's Chest

name: Assassin's Kiss
dropLocation: ToEE: Fire Node, Periron chest (Confirmed), ToEE: Air Node, dragon's hoard chests, ToEE: Water Node, Jotun Rimeblade chest, and ToEE: Earth Node, Hyrelgas chest

name: Atonement
description:
The sword of a repentant soul, his pleadings of forgiveness sharpened and focused
the blade.
dropLocation: Inferno of the Damned, End Chest

name: Audion, the Shattering Way
description: More than glass shatters from the sound of this hammer's strike.
dropLocation: Den of Vipers, end chest

name: Autumn's Veil
description:
FEYTWISTED form of Salazar, Borne of Hemlock 

This leaf is so sharp it can be thrown as if it were a throwing dagger. It
carries the essence of a deep, dark poison, that brings both sleep and death.
dropLocation: The Dryad and the Demigod, Greater feytwisted chest

name: Axe of Adaxus (Level 12)
description:
Legends speak of the first fire giant, Adaxus, whose fiendish alliances led
to the formation of the Sul'at League. Although the knowledge and wisdom of
these giants was lost in the cataclysm that destroyed Xen'drik, the once powerful
leader's axe apparently was not.
dropLocation: Madstone Crater, End chest (Heroic Normal)

description:
This weapon drives those it strikes mad with sensations of starvation, sapping
their strength while simultaneously reversing damage to its wielder's body
and mind.
dropLocation: Harbinger of Madness, End reward

name: Axe of Savaran
description:
A fragment of the Blue Dragon's power that arcs through the air like bolt of
lightning.
dropLocation: Thrall of Duty, end chest

name: Axe of the Scarlet Warrior
description: This greataxe was taken from the body of the Scarlet Warrior.
dropLocation: The Shroud, Part 1

name: Axe of the Unseen Blow
description:
Dwarven smiths crafted this hand axe to be an off-hand companion to a Dwarven
Waraxe. This hand axe is especially well balanced for off hand use reducing
the off hand penalty by 4 instead of the normal 2 for a light weapon.
dropLocation: Litany of the Dead, Cholthulzz Chest, Mentau Chest, High Priest of Vol Chest, Cinnis Chest

name: Balizarde, Protector of the King
description: When hope is lost, only courage remains.
dropLocation: Caught in the Web, End Chest

name: Battleworn Sword
description: This sword once belonged to Gerard Dryden, and has seen much use.
dropLocation: The Crypt of Gerard Dryden, old End Chest

name: Baz'Morath, the Curator of Decay
description:
This unholy greataxe was wielded against you by Rudus Caskrage, a powerful Minotaur.
It can tear the limbs from your enemies' forms and their souls from their bodies.
Its terrible plague will drive your enemies to madness.
dropLocation: Project Nemesis, End Chest

name: Beacon of Day
description:
This mace, named for the great light rising from Stormreach Harbor, longs for
its twin.
dropLocation: The Age of Rage, End chest

name: Beacon of Night
description:
This mace, named for the great light rising from Stormreach Harbor, longs for
its twin.
dropLocation: Night Falls on Stormreach, End Chest

name: Bejeweled Letter Opener
description:
This small knife is bears several gems known to be easily enchanted with healing
properties. A low buzzing sound comes from the knife whenever it is drawn.
dropLocation: The Chamber of Raiyum, Treasure Room Chest

name: Belashyrra's Cleansed Scepter
description:
Though this is but a simple scepter, you feel as though you're holding an instrument
of great power and destruction in your hand as you grip its handle. You find
you cannot weave your spells quite as craftily, but they have become much more
explosive.
dropLocation: The Lord of Eyes, end chest

name: Bereavement
description:
A battle axe created in the Twilight Forge itself. This weapon was forged using
some of the same processes as creating Warforged.
dropLocation: The Titan Awakes, Raid Warded chest

name: Blade of Fury
description:
The signature weapon of a Medusa general from Droaam who attempted to invade
Stormreach. It appears that it allowed her to enter a bloodthirsty rage when
she was sufficiently wounded.
dropLocation: Eyes of Stone, Coin Lords' chest

name: Blade of Inquisition
description: This blade was forged by the Silver Flame to purge undead from the living world.
dropLocation: The Friar's Niece, End chest

name: Blade of the Clouds
description:
Attuned to air and very light in your hands, this will likely serve you quite
well in your travels.
dropLocation: The Promise of Fire, end chest

name: Blade of the High Priestess
description: This blade has been used as an implement in the working of countless dark rituals.
dropLocation: The Portal Opens, End chest ; Menace of the Underdark: City of Portals, Quest chain end reward

name: Bladed Handwraps
description:
The whirling blades of the plane of Shavarath have influenced these Handwraps
with their magic.
dropLocation: Three Paths to Battle, end chest

name: Bleeding Edge
description:
Some wicked blades seek blood. Far more wicked are the blades the blood itself
seeks.
dropLocation: Demon Assault, End chest

name: Blessed Longsword of the Fallen Age
description: Wild magic has imbued entropy into this weapon over the years.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, any end chest
_file: Blessed Longsword of the Fallen Age.item

name: Blessed Longsword of the Golden Age
description: Forged in the glory of Cormanthor, this weapon is remarkably well-preserved.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, rare drop in any end chest
_file: Blessed Longsword of the Golden Age.item

name: Blessed Longsword of the Undying Age
description: The ritual's power has imbued this weapon with an immortal fore and holy purpose.
dropLocation:
Ritual Table, Turn in 
Legendary Blessed Longsword of the Golden Age 
120 Barrier Fragments
_file: Blessed Longsword of the Undying Age.item

name: Blightstaff
description:
Before becoming a prized trophy for House Cannith, this twisted staff of broken
branches was once wielded by a witch with unholy, dangerous powers of disease.
dropLocation: Legendary Vision of Destruction, End chest

name: Blood Machete
description:
The spirits haunting this cursed blade compel the wielder to acts of pure savagery.
Even now, blood still drips from its cutting edge.
dropLocation: Cult of the Six, End reward

name: Bloodknuckles's Smasher
description: This maul was taken from the ogre Bloodknuckles.
dropLocation: The Kobolds' New Ringleader, Bloodknuckle 's Chest

name: Bloodletter's Knife
description:
This wicked serrated knife, once possessed by the kobold Bloodletter, deals
additional slashing damage to foes that are not immune to bleeding.
dropLocation: The Lost Seekers, End reward

name: Bloody Bite
dropLocation: Advance to level 15, End reward

name: Bloody Cleaver
description:
This large blade was once wielded handily by one of the Droaam Army's half-orc
chefs. As one can imagine, it's very well-sharpened and has been used to carry
out many a gruesome deed.
dropLocation: Frame Work, End chests, Dorris ' chests

name: Bloody Hooked Blade
description:
This vicious, curved sword, capable of slicing straight through enemy limbs,
can now grant life to its wielder as it draws blood from his or her foes.
dropLocation: Cauldron of Sora Katra, Upgraded version of Hooked Blade

name: Bloody Shadow Staff
description:
This withered staff, said to be infused with the Shadow's arcane power, can
now grant life to its wielder as it draws blood from his or her foes.
dropLocation: Cauldron of Sora Katra, upgrade of Staff of the Shadow + Bloody Cleaver + Mark of Rhesh Turakbar

name: Bloody War Axe
description:
This war axe, infused with the fury of the Droaam ogre brutes, can now grant
life to its wielder as it draws blood from his or her foes.
dropLocation: Cauldron of Sora Katra, upgraded version of Ogrish War Axe + Bloody Cleaver + Mark of Rhesh Turakbar

name: Bloody War Bow
description:
This powerful gnoll bow can now grant life to its wielder as it draws blood
from his or her foes.
dropLocation: Cauldron of Sora Katra, upgraded version of Gnollish War Bow + Bloody Cleaver + Mark of Rhesh Turakbar

name: Bludgeoning Bankroll
description:
This chunk of transmuted platnium might have been some sort of branch once.
Either way, it's perfect for adventuring.
dropLocation: Another Man's Treasure, end chest

name: Bone Crusher
description:
Infused with a magic which attunes itself to the wielder, the polished stone
staff anticipates movement allowing the heavy weapon to swing effortlessly
through the air.
dropLocation: Palace of Stone, End chest

name: Boneshard Flute
description:
A hollowed-out femur bone inscribed with draconic runes. Voice from beyond the
grave whisper in your ear while you hold the flute.
dropLocation: The Missing Party, End chest

name: Bonesplitter
description:
A grim weapon in its original purpose, the Bonesplitter was made more brutal
still when the influence of Xoriat seeped into it. It deals grievous damage
to most enemies with a skeleton, even severely crippling them on particularly
strong blows.
dropLocation: Hound of Xoriat, Side chest (Heroic Hard or Elite)

name: Booming Longsword of the Fallen Age
description: Wild magic has imbued entropy into this weapon over the years.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, any end chest
_file: Booming Longsword of the Fallen Age.item

name: Bottle o' Rum
description: This broken glass bottle carries quite the sharp edge.
dropLocation: Captive of the Hidden God, end chest

name: Bottled Fear
description:
A glass flask filled with writhing vapor. When it breaks on impact, nearby enemies
are subjected to a Phantasmal Killer effect. (Creates a phantasmal image of
the most fearsome creature imaginable by forming the fears of the enemy's subconscious
mind into something that its conscious mind can visualize. Enemies must make
a Will save to disbelieve the spell and then a Fortitude save or die from fear.
A successful Fortitude save still results in 3d6 damage.
dropLocation: The Night Revels, Turn in 60 Piece of Darkest Chocolate, obtained in the Crypt, in any challenge, and via Draught of Midnight

name: Bottled Fireball
description:
Breaks on impact, dealing fire damage to nearby enemies, scaling with character level.
Also makes a good lamp.
dropLocation: Anniversary Card Collection, Turn in Card I: Kobold & Card IV: Mimic for 50; Card VII: Owlbear & Card X: Tiamat for 100

name: Bottled Flesh to Stone
description:
May turn enemies in an area to stone. (DC check based on character level.)
Instant statue garden, in a bottle!
dropLocation: Anniversary Card Collection, Turn in Card V: Beholder & Card VIII: Dragon for 7; Card I: Kobold & Card IX: Lolth for 15

name: Bottled Healing
description:
Heals nearby players and hirelings at the point of impact. Healing scales with character level.

In Case of Emergency: Break Glass
dropLocation: Anniversary Card Collection, Turn in Card III: Kuo-Toa & Card IX: Lolth for 15 or Card III: Kuo-Toa & Card VI: Grell for 7

name: Bottled Tornado
description:
Summons a howling tempest at the area of impact, dealing area damage over time
and potentially knocking down enemies. Damage based on character level.
dropLocation: Anniversary Card Collection, Turn in Card VI: Grell & Card VII: Owlbear for 7; Card IV: Mimic & Card X: Tiamat for 15

name: Bouquet of Flowers
description:
A fragrant bunch of flowers to celebrate an occasion, show appreciation, or
give to the one you secretly admire. After their twenty charges are used, the
flowers will wilt and disappear.
dropLocation: DDO Store, general buffs

name: Bow of Sinew
description:
A gruesome bow strung with the guts of some foul Xoriat beast. Though it is
repulsive, the sinew it is constructed from makes it a more flexible, speedy
and deadly weapon than other bows of its type.
dropLocation: Harbinger of Madness, End reward

name: Bow of the Elements (Air)
description: A composite longbow containing a bound air elemental.
dropLocation:
Sands of Menechtarun, Undead Area: Whirling Ozann 's chest (Rare Encounter) ; 
 Masei Mkembe sells for 625 Antique Bronze Tokens

name: Bow of the Elements (Ice)
description: A longbow containing a bound water elemental.
dropLocation: Ghosts of Perdition, End chest

name: Bow of the Jade Elf
description: This bow was taken from the body of the Jade Elf, a famous archer.
dropLocation: The Shroud, Phase 1

name: Bow of the Silver Flame
description:
When Templars of the Silver Flame requested a powerful bow for use against the
undead, the Bow of the Silver Flame was the answer. It blunts the ammunition
used with it such that even skeletons will crumble beneath its assault.
dropLocation: Servants of the Overlord, Drow Ritual chest

name: Braisingstar
description:
The hill giant shaman Brawnpits, renowned throughout Gianthold for his haute
cuisine, has been wielding this morningstar to perfect his art. Not only is
it excellent at the task of tenderizing meat, but the heat it gives off can
also cook a carcass to perfection. From the distinctive smell, you suspect
Brawnpits may also have been using it to stir a pot of his trademark stew.
dropLocation: The Fall of Truth, Raid Warded chest (Epic Normal)

name: Brakhash's Axe
description: This magical axe was taken from the chest of the bugbear assassin Brakhash.
dropLocation: The Kobolds' New Ringleader, Brakhash the Vile 's Chest

name: Branch of Vile Curses (Level 13)
description:
Warmbloods are only good for two things. I'll let you guess what those are.'
-Srass, Lizardfolk Mercenary
dropLocation: Escape Plan, End chest (Heroic Normal)

name: Branch of Vile Curses (Level 14)
description:
Warmbloods are only good for two things. I'll let you guess what those are.'
-Srass, Lizardfolk Mercenary
dropLocation: Escape Plan, End chest (Heroic Hard)

name: Branch of Vile Curses (Level 15)
description:
Warmbloods are only good for two things. I'll let you guess what those are.'
-Srass, Lizardfolk Mercenary
dropLocation: Escape Plan, End chest (Heroic Elite)

name: Bravo's Sword
description: This sword is of exquisite construction, rivaling even that of the dwarves.
dropLocation: The Titan Awakes, Raid Warded chest

name: Breach, The Dividing Blade
description:
This massive falchion has emerged in most of the Realm' notable conflicts in
the past century. How it finds its way into the hands of so many different
bearers is unknown, but when brought into battle, few can stand against its
power.
dropLocation: Caught in the Web, End Chest

name: Breeze
description:
This simple hollow staff is surprising sturdy and extremely light. Those that
seek to master it should flow with the wind rather than fight against it. Dexterous
users will find that their agility dramatically increases their ability to
strike and damage, while those that favor brute force will be confounded.
dropLocation: Ascension Chamber, Raid Warded chest

name: Brigand's Cutlass (legacy) (level 12)
description:
A curved sword with an elegant hilt. popular amongst pirates for its intimidating
and yet sophisticated appearance. This particular blade is infused with the
rowdiness and anarchy of a high-seas brigand, and is impeccably useful against
lawful opponents.
dropLocation: Treasure of Crystal Cove, Turn in 800 Copper Doubloon 400 Silver Doubloon 25 Stolen Amethyst at Dusty Old Treasure of Crystal Cove

name: Brilliant Crescent
description:
The broken half a complex staff-like weapon enchanted to allow almost anyone
to use it with some skill. This seems eerily drawn to your [main hand/offhand]...
dropLocation: Killing Time, end chest

name: Brine Shaman's Staff
description:
This staff, made from weathered driftwood, is adorned with small shells and
smells of the sea air. It has the power to summon a strong and sudden gust
of wind, likely used to fill the sails of a ship. This power recharges automatically
as time passes.
dropLocation: Three-Barrel Cove (heroic), End reward

name: Broccoli Bashers
description: As everyone knows, Oozes hate broccoli.
dropLocation: Anniversary Party, Turn in 80

name: Broken Oar (level 12)
description:
You're pretty sure this was an oar once. It doesn't do much to propel a ship
now, but at least you can smack your foes with the pointy end.
dropLocation: Treasure of Crystal Cove, Turn in 500 Silver Doubloon 200 Golden Doubloon 25 Stolen Amethyst 500 Green Dragonshard at Euphonia's Barter Box

name: Bronze Ingot Arcanum (historic)
description:
Once used as a wand by the giants of the Lost Empire, this five-foot rod makes
a heavy fighting staff for a human-sized wielder. It is inscribed with runes
that would fascinate and befuddle the greatest scholars of Xen'drik.
dropLocation: Gianthold Tor, Black Dragon Chest

name: Bullywug Priestess' Staff
description:
The Quarterstaff is imbued with the Cold light of the depths. It appears to
be attuned to profane magic.
dropLocation: The Final Enemy, End Chest

name: Burning Blade of Estelar
description:
A fragment of the Red Dragon's power that manifested in his hoard. It burns
to the touch, but causes no damage to its wielder.
dropLocation: Burning Down the House, end chest

description: Wild magic has imbued entropy into this weapon over the years.

name: Celestia, Brightest Star of Day
description:
This magnificent bastard sword appears to somehow have been made from a ray
of solidified light. It is nearly weightless, and despite its size, is able
to be wielded effectively by anyone who is proficient in the use of short swords.
Rumor and hushed whispers tell of three sunblades among the Stars of Day that
stand out from their brethren, feared by all that hide in shadow and darkness.
This blade is Celestia, the Brightest Star of Day.
dropLocation: Caught in the Web, End Chest

name: Celestial Beacon

a heavy fighting staff for a human-sized wielder. It is inscribed with runes
that would fascinate and befuddle the greatest scholars of Xen'drik.

name: Divine Artillery
description: A more elegant crossbow for a more elegant combatant.
dropLocation: The Devil's Details, End chest

name: Divine Vengeance
description:
This shining, divine blade is of legendary repute, and is said to have been
used by a paladin who ventured into the Subterrane to battle its denizens.
It becomes more powerful when used by those who are devoted to Law, and its
power increases even further when a paladin wields it.
dropLocation: A Vision of Destruction, End chest

name: Divinity, the Morninglord's Goal
description:
It alone will judge you.

This blade becomes more powerful when used by those who are devoted to Law,
and its power increases even further when a paladin wields it.
dropLocation: The Curse of Strahd, Raid Chest

name: Doublecross Bow
description:
The drow of Faerun's Underdark favor small handcrossbows that can be used quickly,
silently, and with poisoned ammunition. The bolts fired from this light repeating
weapon will inject a struck target with a venom that may put it to sleep instantly.
dropLocation: The Spinner of Shadows, End Chest (to the right of The Silver Flame)

description:

did not flee from its fearsome wielder.
dropLocation: Records of the Past, End Chest

description: The holy man marched into the sea. Only his torch returned.
dropLocation: A Legend Revisited, Jack Jibbers ' chest, Three-Barrel Cove (epic), rare encounters

name: Drysvayla's Axe
description:
A fragment of the great Green Dragon's power made manifest, a sad reminder of
a day where reason failed.
dropLocation: Going Rogue, end chest

name: Drysvayla's Razor
description:
A fragment of the great Green Dragon's power made manifest, a sad reminder of
a day where reason failed.
dropLocation: Going Rogue, end chest

name: Duality, The Moral Compass
description: Be better than you ever were.
dropLocation: The Curse of Strahd, Raid chest

name: Dueling Schlager
description:
A sturdy-looking heavy sabre with the point ground down to a razor-sharp tip.
A strong thrust with it could completely skewer an opponent.
dropLocation: The Tear of Dhakaan, Champion Tarkar 's Chest

name: Duelist's Epee
description: This Rapier is ideal for any who fancy themselves a duelist.
dropLocation: The Haunting of Saltmarsh, end chest

name: Duergar Waraxe of the Weapon Master
description:
Gray Dwarves, or Duergar, are often used by drow as slaves or mercenaries. A
Duergar's waraxe is often the last thing seen by a foolhardy adventurer in
the Underdark.
dropLocation: The House of Rusted Blades, End chest
_file: Duergar Waraxe of the Weapon Master.item

name: Dull Dagger
description: This grim implement seems to call out for blood...
dropLocation: Legendary Vision of Destruction, End chest

name: Dusk, the Light Descends
description: The light shines strongest at the end of days.
dropLocation: Old Baba's Hut, Raid Chest

name: Dwarven Brawler's Wraps
description:
Legend has it that when a great Dwarven hero had his axe shattered by the Drow,
he took a swig from his flask, tore his tunic, wrapped the shreds around his
hands, and routed them with nothing but devastating punches.
dropLocation: Desire in the Dark, End chest

name: Dwarven Thrower
description: This throwing axe is of obviously dwarven make.
dropLocation: The Subterrane - Central, Garamol 's chest

name: Dwarven Waraxe of the Fallen Age
description: Wild magic has imbued entropy into this weapon over the years.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, any end chest
_file: Dwarven Waraxe of the Fallen Age.item

name: Dwarven Waraxe of the Golden Age
description: Forged in the glory of Cormanthor, this weapon is remarkably well-preserved.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, rare drop in any end chest
_file: Dwarven Waraxe of the Golden Age.item

name: Dwarven Waraxe of the Undying Age
description: The ritual's power has imbued this weapon with an immortal fire and unholy purpose.
dropLocation:
Ritual Table, Turn in 
Legendary Dwarven Waraxe of the Golden Age 
120 Barrier Fragments
_file: Dwarven Waraxe of the Undying Age.item

name: Dynastic Falcata
description: A fine falcata forged for the sons of kings.
dropLocation:
Sands of Menechtarun, Undead area - General Tanankh 's chest (Rare Encounter) ; 
 Masei Mkembe sells for 625 Antique Bronze Tokens

name: Earthcarver Shard
description:
This appears to be merely the broken shard of a much more powerful implement.
Nevertheless, it's extremely effective against purple worms and the digestive
slimes that inhabit their stomachs.
dropLocation: In the Belly of the Beast, Special quest only temporary weapon

name: Earthcarver
description:
Now fully reassembled, this massive blade is unmatched against purple worms
and the creatures that crawl through their guts.
dropLocation: In the Belly of the Beast, Special quest only temporary weapon

name: Earthshatter Warhammer
description:
A tremendous blunt weapon infused with the power of Orlassk, the daelkyr Lord
of Stone. It can trap enemies in stone, then shatter that stone forcefully
with adamantine-reinforced blows.
dropLocation: The Lord of Stone, End chest

name: Echo of Blackrazor
description: It delights in your voracity.
dropLocation: White Plume Mountain, End chest

name: Echo of the Artblade
description:
An echo of the legendary elven longsword Artblade. This echo broke off of the
Artblade's mind in defiance of the Blade-rite's draconian tendencies, and is
open to being wielded by anyone.
dropLocation: Portal to Below, end chest (rare)

name: Echo of the Crownblade
description:
An echo of the legendary elven longsword Ruler's Blade, also known as the Crownblade.
This echo broke off of the Crownblade's mind in defiance of the Blade-rite's
draconian tendencies, and is open to being wielded by anyone.
dropLocation: The Zulkir's Plan, end chest (rare)

name: Echo of the Sunsword
description:
This blade, like the one that ended Strahd, shines with a brilliant glow. It's
not the same, but it's close.
dropLocation: The Curse of Strahd, Raid Warded Chest

name: Echo of the Warblade
description:
An echo of the legendary elven longsword Warblade. This echo broke off of the
Warblade's mind in defiance of the Blade-rite's draconian tendencies, and is
open to being wielded by anyone.
dropLocation: The Key to the Mythal, end chest (rare)

name: Echo of Wave
description:
The crash of the waves echoes in your ears. Seize the moment, and swim for the
surface.
dropLocation: White Plume Mountain, End chest

name: Echo of Whelm
description: Steely strikes and brewing storms
dropLocation: White Plume Mountain, end chest

name: Eidolon of the Shadow
description:
FEYTWISTED form of Esper, the Shadowblade 

This manifestation of Illusion Magic is made real by your touch... and something
else. As the Shadowblade is not physical, monks may use it without becoming
uncentered.
dropLocation: The Dryad and the Demigod, Greater feytwisted chest

description:
FEYTWISTED form of Aeon, the Blazing Reign 

Fallen through time and twisted by Fey magics, this Longbow memorializes
the reign of a mighty Green Dracolich that... never was.
dropLocation: Year of the Dragon, Draconic Raider's Reward Box

name: Epic Ancient Vulkoorim Dagger
description:
An ancient dagger forged in the days of the elven revolt against the giants.
This dagger has a small chance to kill any giant outright.
dropLocation: Epic version of Ancient Vulkoorim Dagger

name: Epic Antique Greataxe
description:
Lamden Wynn of the Golden Wing Inn kept this greataxe behind the bar in case
of trouble. It's an old family heirloom, but he offered it to you as compensation
for your efforts.
dropLocation: Epic version of Antique Greataxe

name: Epic Arcanum Stave
description: This magical weapon channels arcane energy through you.
dropLocation: Endgame: Marguerite, End chest

name: Epic Assassin's Kiss
dropLocation: ToEE: Fire Node, Periron chest (Confirmed), ToEE: Air Node, dragon's hoard chests, ToEE: Water Node, Jotun Rimeblade chest, and ToEE: Earth Node, Hyrelgas chest

name: Epic Bejeweled Letter Opener
description:
This small knife is bears several gems known to be easily enchanted with healing
properties. A low buzzing sound comes from the knife whenever it is drawn.
dropLocation: Epic version of Bejeweled Letter Opener

name: Epic Blade of Inquisition
description: This blade was forged by the Silver Flame to purge undead from the living world.
dropLocation: The Friar's Niece, End chest

name: Epic Bleeding Edge
description:
Some wicked blades seek blood. Far more wicked are the blades the blood itself
seeks.
dropLocation: Demon Assault, End chest

name: Epic Bludgeoning Bankroll
description:
This chunk of transmuted platnium might have been some sort of branch once.
Either way, it's perfect for adventuring.
dropLocation: Another Man's Treasure, End chest

name: Epic Bone Crusher
description:
Infused with a magic which attunes itself to the wielder, the polished stone
staff anticipates movement allowing the heavy weapon to swing effortlessly
through the air.
dropLocation: Palace of Stone, End chest

name: Epic Boneshard Flute
description:
A hollowed-out femur bone inscribed with draconic runes. Voices from beyond
the grave whisper in your ear while you hold the flute.
dropLocation: The Missing Party, End chest

name: Epic Bow of the Elements (Air)
description: A composite longbow containing a bound air elemental.
dropLocation: Epic version of Bow of the Elements (Air)

name: Epic Bow of the Silver Flame
description:
When Templars of the Silver Flame requested a powerful bow for use against the
undead, the Bow of the Silver Flame was the answer. It blunts the ammunition
used with it such that even skeletons will crumble beneath its assault.
dropLocation: Epic version of Bow of the Silver Flame
_file: Epic Bow of the Silver Flame.item

A curved sword with an elegant hilt, popular amongst pirates for its intimidating
and yet sophisticated appearance. This particular blade is infused with the
rowdiness and anarchy of a high-seas brigand, and is impeccably useful against
lawful opponents.
dropLocation: Treasure of Crystal Cove, Turn in 1200 Copper Doubloon 600 Silver Doubloon 35 Stolen Emerald at Dusty Old Treasure of Crystal Cove

name: Epic Templar's Justice
description:
A sanctified axe that shines brightly with the power of the Silver Flame. It
is said to deliver the wrath of Tira Miron again and again upon those that
it strikes.
dropLocation: Epic version of Templar's Justice

name: Epic Templar's Retribution
description:
A sanctified blade that shines brightly with the power of the Silver Flame.
It is said to deliver the wrath of Tira Miron again and again upon those that
it strikes.
dropLocation: Epic version of Templar's Retribution

name: Epic Thornlord
description:
A longbow made from many wooden scraps scavenged from thorn bushes. Enchantments
make this bow exceptionally easy to pull and fire resulting in an additional
+4 damage.
dropLocation: Epic version of Thornlord

name: Epic Thrall of the Fire Temple
description: "There are benefits to submitting to the path of flame." ~Alrrem
dropLocation: ToEE: Fire Node, Periron chest (Confirmed), ToEE: Air Node, dragon's hoard chests, ToEE: Water Node, Jotun Rimeblade chest, and ToEE: Earth Node, Hyrelgas chest
_file: Epic Thrall of the Fire Temple.item

name: Epic Timeblade
description:
A sword gifted with the power to bend time. Powerful blows against your enemies
will slow their actions, and if you are falling and in danger it will slow
your descent so that you land safely.
dropLocation: Epic version of Timeblade

name: Epic Tinder
description:
This long staff is imbued with the burning power of the desert sun. Fire Spells
cast using this staff to focus them ate much more powerful.
dropLocation: Epic version of Tinder

name: Epic Unkor's Cleaver
description:
The blade on this enormous meat cleaver is pitted and scarred by use. Chunks
of meat occasionally get stuck on the blade.
dropLocation: Epic version of Unkor's Cleaver

name: Epic Whirlwind
description:
An air spirit bound to this greatsword shrieks when the sword is swept through
the air. Swirling gusts of wind can knock the sword's target to the ground.
dropLocation: Epic version of Whirlwind

name: Epic Xuum
description: This oversized falchion is a prized possession of Vorvand Darkfur.
dropLocation: Epic version of Xuum

name: Epic Zephyr
description:
A fine steel shortsword containing a bound air elemental. The elemental fights
against those that try to wield it with brute force.
dropLocation: Epic version of Zephyr

name: Erosion, the Crumbling Arcana
description: The burning acid coating the edge of this blade never seems to be exhausted.
dropLocation: Den of Vipers, end chest

name: Esper, the Shadowblade
description:
This manifestation of Illusion Magic is made real by your touch... and something
else. As the Shadowblade is not physical, monks may use it without becoming
uncentered.
dropLocation: The Dryad and the Demigod, End chest and optional chest

name: Eviscerating Expenses
description:
You're pretty sure this sharp khopesh won't turn you to platinum, too. Pretty
sure.
dropLocation: Another Man's Treasure, End chest

name: Falchion of the Fallen Age
description: Wild magic has imbued entropy into this weapon over the years.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, any end chest

name: Falchion of the Golden Age
description: Forged in the glory of Cormanthor, this weapon is remarkably well-preserved.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, rare drop in any end chest

name: Falchion of the Undying Age
description: The ritual's power has imbued this weapon with an immortal fire and unholy purpose.
dropLocation:
Ritual Table, Turn in 
Legendary Falchion of the Golden Age 
120 Barrier Fragments

name: Fang of Siberys
description:
A powerful and fear-inspiring blade that, though it is not in any way forged
from the tooth of a dragon, is said to hold some small measure of the might
of the Dragon Above. It visits the sky's wrath upon its foes.
dropLocation: Reign of Madness, End reward

name: Fanged Wraps
description: These crude handwraps are lined with the teeth of various predators.
dropLocation: Assault on Splinterskull, End reward

name: Fangs of the Wild Hunt
description: Some aspect of the ferocity of the Feywild exists through this curious collar
dropLocation: The Feywild, rare encounters

name: Fate, the Knower of All
description: Feeling lucky?
dropLocation: Old Baba's Hut, Raid Chest

name: Fell Rapier of Ice
description:
An extremely lightweight rapier sheathed in ice. Counts as a Light Weapon for
Two Weapon Fighting purposes.
dropLocation: Ghosts of Perdition, End chest

name: Fellblade (falchion)
description:
This heavy falchion comes from Ghola-Fan's personal collection. Its design excels
at sweeping enemies' feet out from under them. While very effective and well-made,
the weapon shows few signs of use.
dropLocation: Bring Me the Head of Ghola-Fan!, Balcony Chest

name: Fellblade (longsword)
dropLocation: Defiler of the Just, Warded chest

name: Fernian Wraps
description:
These charred wraps survived a Fernian conjunction, and absorbed some of the
plane of fire's power.
dropLocation: The Abandoned Excavation, End reward

name: Forester's Brush Hook (Level 15)
description:
A hooked blade attached perpendicular to a wooden haft, this kind of weapon
is used to clear thick brush or undergrowth when travelling in a dense forest.
It is similar enough to a kama to be wielded expertly by someone who is proficient
with one. This blade seems to have been enchanted to adapt to whatever material
you are trying to cut with it, bypassing most forms of damage reduction.
dropLocation:
Overgrowth, Wood Woad chest (Heroic Normal) ; 
 The Druid's Deep, Quest chain end reward (Heroic & Epic)

name: Forester's Brush Hook (Level 17)
description:
A hooked blade attached perpendicular to a wooden haft, this kind of weapon
is used to clear thick brush or undergrowth when travelling in a dense forest.
It is similar enough to a kama to be wielded expertly by someone who is proficient
with one. This blade seems to have been enchanted to adapt to whatever material
you are trying to cut with it, bypassing most forms of damage reduction.
dropLocation: Overgrowth, Wood Woad chest (Heroic Elite)

name: Forge
description:
In years long passed, Dwarf blacksmiths used hammers such as this to forge the
very blades that are still wielded against the Drow.
dropLocation: Strike Back, End chest

name: Forgeblade
description: As you shall be my sword, let this blade serve as yours.
dropLocation: Advance to level 15, End reward

name: Forgotten Axe
description:
This small stone axe looks like it was forgotten for a long time. If you had
to estimate, eleven years or so. That's a long time to forget about a Hand
Axe.
dropLocation: Black and Blue, end chest

name: Forgotten Light (Level 16)
description: This ancient mace radiates a feeling of peace and safety.
dropLocation: Rest Stop, End chest (Heroic Normal)

description: This ancient mace radiates a feeling of peace and safety.

description:
Worry not when you find yourself chilled to the bone. Worry when you stop feeling
it.
dropLocation: A Break In the Ice, End chest (Heroic Normal)

name: Frostbrand
description:
This great sword is instilled with the essence of Ice. It protects its wielder
from fire damage as well as doing additional cold damage.
dropLocation: The Subterrane - Central, Garamol 's chest

name: Frozen Fish Stick
dropLocation: Sleeping with the Fishes, end chest

name: Frozen Sword Fish
description: This sword seems pretty fishy... or does this fish seemy swordy?
dropLocation: Too Many Cooks, end chest

name: Fury of the Flame
description:
A mighty weapon forged by request of the Silver Flame templars and imbued with
the fiery wrath of the Silver Flame itself. It metes out judgement in the form
of blinding light, burns, and broken bones.
dropLocation: Servants of the Overlord, Flamefang optional chest

name: Fusible Sceptre of Combustion
description: This magical sceptre is attuned to Fire spells.
dropLocation: Beautiful Nightmares, End chest

name: Fusible Sceptre of Corrosion
description: This magical sceptre is attuned to Acid spells.
dropLocation: An Element of Chaos, End chest

name: Fusible Sceptre of Glaciation
description: This magical sceptre is attuned to Cold spells.
dropLocation: Eye Know Whodunnit, End chest

name: Fusible Sceptre of Impulse
description: This magical sceptre is attuned to Force spells.
dropLocation: Beautiful Nightmares, End chest

name: Fusible Sceptre of Magnetism
description: This magical sceptre is attuned to Electric spells.
dropLocation: Eye Know Whodunnit, End chest

name: Garos' Malice
description:
A shortsword imbued with the essence of a powerful red abishai . It assails
those it is wielded against with flames, and its attacks on unaware targets
are stronger.
dropLocation: The Snitch, Garos ' chest (does not drop on epic difficulties)needs verification

name: Garzuul's Bane
description:
A great axe infused with Garzuul's essence and blessed by the Sovereign Host,
this axe will prove to be a powerful weapon against the demons.
dropLocation: Invaders!, End Chest (only if Garzuul is the end boss)

name: Gerard Dryden's Mace
description: This mace once belonged to Gerard Dryden .
dropLocation: The Crypt of Gerard Dryden, Locked Room chest

name: Ghostbane Longbow
description:
This longbow was enchanted for Church of the Silver Flame to give to warriors
that had distinguished themselves in combat against Evil. Arrows fired from
this bow will find their target, even if it is a non-corporeal undead.
dropLocation: Necropolis, Turn in Eerie Scarab Powder

name: Giant Stalker's Knife
description:
This heavy, curved knife was created by the elves of Xen'drik to us in their
rebellion against the giants.
dropLocation: Gwylan's Stand, Suropox 's chest

name: Giant's Fist (Level 17)
description:
Puny little man
You wish to wield a giant's strength?
You must swing harder.'
- Mug, Hill
Giant Defender

name: Gloaming Philter
description:
Doubly-enchanted glass containing pure water from a Shadowfell spring. Breaks on impact, dealing 10 to 60 Negative Energy Damage to nearby enemies, and possibly inflicting a negative level on them.

Caution: Do Not Drink.
dropLocation: The Ruins of Thunderholme, Any Rare Encounter chests

name: Gnashtooth Sickle
description:
The Gnashtooth Kobolds routinely use these weapons in battle versus Troglodytes.
The Troglodyte stench seeps into the metal and may make some enemies nauseous.
dropLocation: The Lost Seekers, End reward

name: Gnollish War Bow
description:
Gnoll archers are sought throughout Droaam for their skilled marksmanship. This
powerful bow, which lets the user apply a greater amount of his or her strength
to the launch of its arrows, is equally sought-after.
dropLocation: Attack on Stormreach, End reward

name: Goldcursed Dagger
description: This dagger bears a valuable curse.
dropLocation: Back to Basics, end chest

name: Grave Wrappings
description:
These strips of cloth previously adorned a denizen of Eveningstar's Deep Glen
Graveyard. After being exposed to Drow necromancy, they are left imbued with
a powerful and evil energy.
dropLocation:
The Unquiet Graves, End chest ;
Menace of the Underdark: The Darkening, Quest chain end reward

name: Great Axe of the Chained Soldier (historic)
description:
The Ancient giants commanded legions of martial drow slaves. These enchanted
arms fortified courage and resolve, and dissuaded the conscripts from flight.
dropLocation: Gianthold Tor, White Dragon Chest
_file: Great Axe of the Chained Soldier (historic).item

name: Greatbow of the Scrag
description:
Once carried by the fearsome Scrag of the Three-Barrel Cove, Dulse, this longbow
has a powerful draw, able to fire arrows with enough force to not only pierce
the flesh, but even shatter bone.
dropLocation: Three-Barrel Cove (heroic), Dulse 's chest (Rare Encounter)

name: Greatclub of the Fallen Age
description: Wild magic has imbued entropy into this weapon over the years.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, any end chest

name: Greatclub of the Golden Age
description: Forged in the glory of Cormanthor, this weapon is remarkably well-preserved.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, rare drop in any end chest

name: Greatclub of the Scrag
description:
This particularly nasty weapon once belonged to one of the Scrag in High Priestess'
Slikir's service. It seems that when the troll was killed, its blood seeped
into this club, granting the weapon some of the creature's characteristics.
dropLocation: Altar of Vulkoor, Cleansed version of Dampened Greatclub of the Scrag

name: Greatclub of the Undying Age
description: The ritual's power has imbued this weapon with an immortal fire and unholy purpose.
dropLocation:
Ritual Table, Turn in

120 Barrier Fragments

name: Greater Vial of Seafog
description:
A glass vial filled with water and thick mist. When it breaks on impact, a bank
of Solid Fog is created on the enemy it struck.
dropLocation: Treasure of Crystal Cove, Turn in 25 gold doubloons at Euphonia's Barter Box

name: Greatsword of the Fallen Age
description: Wild magic has imbued entropy into this weapon over the years.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, any end chest

name: Green Blade
description:
A simple knife with a curved green tinged blade and a handle of polished densewood.
Holding the dagger, you feel a rush of arcane power. This is no mere dagger.
dropLocation: Zawabi's Revenge, Warded Chest

name: Green Steel Bastard Sword
description:
Also known as a hand-and-a-half sword, and the largest of the single-handed
swords, this weapon requires special trainiing to use.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Battle Axe
description: A sturdy, broad-headed axe used as a weapon especially among dwarves.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Dagger
description: A short simple weapon with a pointed blade used for stabbing.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Dwarven Waraxe
description:
This axe is too large to be used in one hand without the special training that
all dwarven warriors receive.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Falchion
description:
This large, curved sword is essentially a scimitar designed to be used with
two hands.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Great Crossbow
description:
The largest of the crossbows, it is weighty enough to require special training
to use.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Greataxe
description: A big, heavy, double-bladed axe wieldable only with two hands.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Greatclub
description:
A more formidable version of a simple club, this two-handed weapon is occasionally
reinforced with spikes or bands of iron.
dropLocation: Special event items, Festivult (Gold Coin Rewards)

name: Green Steel Greatsword
description: The largest of the swords, this weapon can only be used with two hands.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Hand Axe
description: A smaller version of the Battleaxe, favored as a offhand weapon by the dwarves.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Handwraps
description:
Strips of cloth meant to be wrapped around your hands. They have small pieces
of a foreign metal woven throughout.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Heavy Crossbow
description:
A ranged weapon consisting of a small bow mounted crosswise on a heavy wooden
stock. This weapon is large enough to require a winch for reloading, yet is
surprisingly simple to use.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Heavy Mace
description: A smaller simple weapon comprised of a solid blunt head attached to a haft.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Heavy Pick
description:
A hammer with a sharp, pointed head designed to concentrate the force of its
blow on a small area. Similar to a miner's pick, but reinforced for battle.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Kama
description:
A weapon with a straight inside edge attached perpendicularly to a thin wooden
haft and wielded with one hand.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Khopesh
description: An ancient sword with a heavy spine and a single blade.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Kukri
description: A heavy, curved knife with a shard edge on the inside of the curve.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Light Crossbow
description:
A simple ranged weapon consisting of a small bow mounted crosswise on a light
wooden stock.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Light Hammer
description: A small sledge with a blunt, square head.
dropLocation: Special event items, Festivult (Gold Coin Rewards)

name: Green Steel Light Mace
description: A smaller simple weapon comprised of a solid blunt head attached to a haft.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Light Pick
description:
A small hammer with a sharp, pointed head designed to concentrate the force
of its blow on a small area. Similar to a miner's pick, but smaller and reinforced
for battle.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Longbow
description:
A large, curved wooden bow, often nearly as tall as a human. This weapon is
popular among elves and rangers.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Longsword
description:
A classic straight-bladed sword usable in one hand. This weapon is commonly
associated with knighthood and valor.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Maul
description:
The largest of the hammers, often created with a wedge-shaped head for splitting
logs.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Morningstar
description: A simple weapon comprised of a round, spiked head and a long haft.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Quarterstaff
description:
A long, stout staff used as a simple weapon. Often favored by wizards or common
folk.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Rapier
description:
A long, thin sword with a fine point. Unlike other swords, this weapon lacks
an edged blade, and can only be used for thrusting attacks.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Repeating Heavy Crossbow
description:
A complex weapon consisting of a heavy crossbow and a clip for bolts that is
loaded by pulling the lever.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Repeating Light Crossbow
description:
A complex weapon consisting of a light crossbow and a clip for bolts that is
loaded by pulling the reloading lever.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Sceptre
description: A simple weapon with a wide, round head and a narrow haft.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Scimitar
description:
A curved sword favored by the elves of Valenar. The unique shape gives this
weapon a keener edge.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Shortbow
description: A smaller wooden bow with a shorter firing distance.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Shortsword
description:
The smallest of the swords, light enough to be used as an offhand weapon, but
too large to be considered a dagger.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Shuriken
description:
A tiny circular throwing weapon with a serrated edge. Favored by the plentiful
ninjas of Shavarath.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Sickle
description:
A simple weapon with a semicircular blade attached to a short handle. Typically
used by farmers for cutting grain.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Throwing Axe
description: A small, light axe design for throwing.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Throwing Dagger
description: A short simple weapon with a pointed blade balanced for throwing.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Throwing Hammer
description: A small, light hammer designed for throwing.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Green Steel Warhammer
description:
A large sledge with a heavy, square head. Popular among dwarves as an alternative
to the axe.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Greenstone Dagger
description: This green stone has crystallized into something deadly.
dropLocation: One Dame Thing After Another, end chest

name: Greenstone Greatsword
description: This green stone has crystallized into something deadly.
dropLocation: Through the Tulgey Wood, locked chest, end chest

name: Grievous Blade
description:
This dagger is a cruel looking instrument. The notched blade is designed to
leave a ragged, ill-healing wound. A well placed strike will cause much blood
loss.
dropLocation: The Tear of Dhakaan, Champion Tarkar 's chest

name: Grix's Gouger
description: It's best not to think about the violence that this crystalline Pick has enabled.
dropLocation: Hunt or Be Hunted, end chest

name: Guardian of the Liturgy
description:
This dagger was once used in the blood rituals to Vol performed in this ancient
shrine.
dropLocation: Desecrated Temple of Vol, End Chest

name: Guardian's Dagger
description:
This menacing sword was found on the body of the Guardian of Shan-To-Kor . To
him, it was a tiny dagger, great for throwing with precision at unwary adversaries.
To you, it is a razor-sharp Great Sword.
dropLocation: The Seal of Shan-To-Kor, End reward

name: Guardkiller Bow
description:
Krung the Guard-Killer was feared by the Stormreach Guards. Not anymore. His
legacy, however, lives on in the fearsome bow.
dropLocation: The Lost Seekers, End reward

name: Guidance
description: The crossbow Guidance is loathed by the enemies of the Silver Flame.
dropLocation: The Old Archives, End chest (by the third Fragment)

name: Gulthias Staff
description: Trust none who serve but the Mother Night.
dropLocation: Old Baba's Hut, Raid Chest

name: Gwylan's Blade
description:
This shortsword was carried by the ancient elf hero Gwylan in his final stand
against the giant lords.
dropLocation: Gwylan's Stand, Zared Mightyhoof 's chest

description: This intricate weapon bears the mark of House Cannith.
dropLocation: Legendary Master Artificer, Either End chest

name: Legendary Ancient Vulkoorim Dagger
description:
An ancient dagger forged in the days of the elven revolt against the giants.
This dagger has a small chance to kill any giant outright.
dropLocation: Legendary version of Epic Ancient Vulkoorim Dagger

name: Legendary Antique Greataxe
description:
Lamden Wynn of the Golden Wing Inn kept this greataxe behind the bar in case
of trouble. It's an old family heirloom, but he offered it to you as compensation
for your efforts.
dropLocation: Legendary version of Epic Antique Greataxe

name: Legendary Arc Ricochet
description: Lightning flickers between the barbs on this shuriken.
dropLocation: Ruinous Schemes, End chest

name: Legendary Axe of Savaran
description:
A fragment of the Blue Dragon's power that arcs through the air like bolt of
lightning.
dropLocation: Thrall of Duty, end chest

name: Legendary Ball o' Yarn
dropLocation: Catastrophe, end chest

name: Legendary Bastard Sword of the Fallen Age
description: Wild magic has imbued entropy into this weapon over the years.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, any end chest
_file: Legendary Bastard Sword of the Fallen Age.item

name: Legendary Bastard Sword of the Golden Age
description: Forged in the glory of Cormanthor, this weapon is remarkably well-preserved.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, rare drop in any end chest

name: Legendary Beacon of Day
description:
This mace, named for the great light rising from Stormreach Harbor, longs for
its twin.
dropLocation: The Age of Rage, End chest

name: Legendary Beacon of Night
description:
This mace, named for the great light rising from Stormreach Harbor, longs for
its twin.
dropLocation: Night Falls on Stormreach, End Chest

name: Legendary Bejeweled Letter Opener
description:
This small knife is bears several gems known to be easily enchanted with healing
properties. A low buzzing sound comes from the knife whenever it is drawn.
dropLocation: Legendary version of Epic Bejeweled Letter Opener

name: Legendary Blade of Fury
description:
The signature weapon of a Medusa general from Droaam who attempted to invade
Stormreach. It appears that it allowed her to enter a bloodthirsty rage when
she was sufficiently wounded.
dropLocation: Eyes of Stone, Coin Lords' chest

name: Legendary Blade of the Clouds
description:
Attuned to air and very light in your hands, this will likely serve you quite
well in your travels.
dropLocation: The Promise of Fire, end chest

name: Legendary Bladed Handwraps
description:
The whirling blades of the plane of Shavarath have influenced these Handwraps
with their magic.
dropLocation: Three Paths to Battle, end chest

name: Legendary Blessed Longsword of the Fallen Age
description: Wild magic has imbued entropy into this weapon over the years.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, any end chest
_file: Legendary Blessed Longsword of the Fallen Age.item

name: Legendary Blessed Longsword of the Golden Age
description: Forged in the glory of Cormanthor, this weapon is remarkably well-preserved.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, rare drop in any end chest
_file: Legendary Blessed Longsword of the Golden Age.item

name: Legendary Bloody Cleaver
description:
This large blade was once wielded handily by one of the Droaam Army's half-orc
chefs. As one can imagine, it's very well-sharpened and has been used to carry
out many a gruesome deed.
dropLocation: Frame Work, End chests, Dorris ' chests

name: Legendary Bloody Hooked Blade
description:
This vicious, curved sword, capable of slicing straight through enemy limbs,
can now grant life to its wielder as it draws blood from his or her foes.
dropLocation: Cauldron of Sora Katra, upgraded version of Legendary Hooked Blade

name: Legendary Bloody Shadow Staff
description:
This withered staff, said to be infused with the Shadow's arcane power, can
now grant life to its wielder as it draws blood from his or her foes.
dropLocation: Cauldron of Sora Katra, upgraded version of Legendary Staff of the Shadow

name: Legendary Bloody War Axe
description:
This war axe, infused with the fury of the Droaam ogre brutes, can now grant
life to its wielder as it draws blood from his or her foes.
dropLocation: Cauldron of Sora Katra, upgraded version of Legendary Ogrish War Axe

name: Legendary Bloody War Bow
description:
This powerful gnoll bow can now grant life to its wielder as it draws blood
from his or her foes.
dropLocation: Cauldron of Sora Katra, upgraded version of Legendary Gnollish War Bow

name: Legendary Booming Longsword of the Fallen Age
description: Wild magic has imbued entropy into this weapon over the years.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, any end chest
_file: Legendary Booming Longsword of the Fallen Age.item

description: Forged in the glory of Cormanthor, this weapon is remarkably well-preserved.
dropLocation: Magic of Myth Drannor, rare drop in any end chest
_file: Legendary Booming Longsword of the Golden Age.item

name: Legendary Bottle o' Rum
description: This broken glass bottle carries quite the sharp edge.
dropLocation: Captive of the Hidden God, end chest

name: Legendary Bow of the Elements (Air)
description: A composite longbow containing a bound air elemental.
dropLocation: Legendary version of Epic Bow of the Elements (Air)

name: Legendary Bow of the Silver Flame
description:
When Templars of the Silver Flame requested a powerful bow for use against the
undead, the Bow of the Silver Flame was the answer. It blunts the ammunition
used with it such that even skeletons will crumble beneath its assault.
dropLocation: Legendary version of Epic Bow of the Silver Flame
_file: Legendary Bow of the Silver Flame.item

name: Legendary Brigand's Cutlass (level 32)
description:
A curved sword with an elegant hilt, popular amongst pirates for its intimidating
and yet sophisticated appearance. This particular blade is infused with the
rowdiness and anarchy of a high-seas brigand, and is impeccably useful against
lawful opponents.
dropLocation: Treasure of Crystal Cove, Turn in 1500 Silver Doubloon 700 Golden Doubloon 45 Stolen Jasper 1000 Green Dragonshard at Euphonia's Barter Box

name: Legendary Broken Oar (level 32)
description:
You're pretty sure this was an oar once. It doesn't do much to propel a ship
now, but at least you can smack your foes with the pointy end.
dropLocation: Treasure of Crystal Cove, Turn in 1500 Silver Doubloon 700 Golden Doubloon 45 Stolen Jasper 1000 Green Dragonshard at Euphonia's Barter Box

name: Legendary Bullywug Priestess' Staff
description:
This Quarterstaff is imbued with the Cold light of the depths. It appears to
be attuned to profane magic.
dropLocation: The Final Enemy, end chest, or Legendary version of Epic Bullywug Priestess' Staff

name: Legendary Burning Blade of Estelar
description:
A fragment of the Red Dragon's power that manifested in his hoard. It burns
to the touch, but causes no damage to its wielder.
dropLocation: Burning Down the House, end chest

description:
This sceptre radiates brilliant light. It's painful to look at, but at least
you'll have trouble losing it.
dropLocation: White Plume Mountain, Burket and Snarla's chest

description: A sword wielded by the demonic Marilith.
dropLocation: Legendary version of Epic Chaosblade

it rumored to have powers that can only be used by those that bear the Mark
of Sentinel.

name: Legendary Collapsible Shortbow
description:
A small bow ingeniously jointed to collapse into a small pack. Wielding this
bow, allows a rogue to perform prodigious athletic feats. Arrows fired from
this bow gain magical barbs of force, dealing slashing and piercing damage.
dropLocation: Legendary version of Epic Collapsible Shortbow

name: Legendary Crippling Cleaver
description:
This falchion, infused with the power to draw a living enemy's blood, can now
cripple an enemy and hamper its movements.
dropLocation: Cauldron of Sora Katra, upgraded version of Legendary Bloody Cleaver

name: Legendary Crippling Fury Blade
description:
This large sword, capable of conferring a disease to enemies, can now cripple
an enemy and hamper its movement.
dropLocation: Cauldron of Sora Katra, upgraded version of Legendary Blade of Fury

name: Legendary Crippling Fury Shortsword
description:
This small sword, capable of conferring a disease to enemies, can now cripple
an enemy and hamper its movement.
dropLocation: Cauldron of Sora Katra, upgraded version of Legendary Blade of Fury (produces a pair of swords)

name: Legendary Crippling Rocksplitter
description:
This pick, said to be infused with the Shadow's arcane power, can now cripple
an enemy and hamper its movements.
dropLocation: Cauldron of Sora Katra, upgraded version of Legendary Rocksplitter

description:

an enemy and hamper its movement.
dropLocation: Cauldron of Sora Katra, upgraded version of Legendary Stonedust Handwraps

name: Legendary Crochet Crusher
description: Hurts more than you'd think yarn would.
dropLocation: Catastrophe, end chest

name: Legendary Crocodile Maw
description: Crocodile Fangs stud these Handwraps.
dropLocation: Swim at Your Own Risk, end chest, or Legendary version of Epic Crocodile Maw

name: Legendary Cruel Nobility
description:
A finely wrought scimitar that once belonged to a Rakshasa noble, glowing darkly
with an unholy aura. It tears into enemies visciously and is especially debilitating
against those whose hearts are not evil.
dropLocation: Legendary version of Epic Cruel Nobility

name: Legendary Cutthroat's Smallblade (level 32)
description:
When a cutlass is simply too big for the job, a pirate will turn instead to
a trusty smallblade. This one is serrated and quite adept at inflicting painful
wounds on living creatures.
dropLocation: Treasure of Crystal Cove, Turn in 1500 Silver Doubloon 700 Golden Doubloon 45 Stolen Jasper 1000 Green Dragonshard at Euphonia's Barter Box
